title: NA-Non ULIP
word_text:
Critical Illness Cover: Protect Yourself Against Major Health Risks
Added Word Count: 798
URL: https://www.bajajallianzlife.com/life-insurance-guide/health/all-about-critical-illness-cover.html
Being healthy is crucial to achieving any life goal. While one part of being healthy is exercising and eating healthy, the other element is to financially prepare for any illness or eventuality.
The increasing incidences of Non-Communicable Diseases (NCDs) in India further make it essential to have one plan for critical illnesses. According to the World Health Organization, Non-communicable diseases (NCDs) kill 41 million people each year, equivalent to 74% of all deaths globally1. NCDs include critical illnesses like heart diseases, cancer, diabetes, and chronic kidney diseases, among others.
Detection of a critical illness in you or a family member is not only emotionally debilitating but also financially draining for the entire family and can derail your life goals. With the cost of healthcare expenses increasing almost at the double the pace of inflation, the financial burden that critical illness in the family brings can be as challenging to deal with as the emotional trauma. However, you can always plan ahead for such financial stress.
What Is A Critical Illness?
A critical Illness can be one of the many serious ailments, which require lengthy medical procedures, including surgeries, and other pre- and post-hospitalization care to be fully cured. Some of them can be chronic in nature and may require lifelong medication and treatment. These medical procedures and treatments, many of which are only conducted in large private hospitals, cost a fortune.
Add to this the cost of medication, regular follow-ups with doctors and alternative treatments and one critical illness in a family can cause irreparable damage to your finances, if you have not taken additional protection.
What is Critical Illness Benefit?
The critical illness benefit is an optional rider available under some life insurance plans, that pays a pre-defined sum assured upon the diagnosis of a critical illness, listed under the plan. This rider enhances the protection under the base plan to offer a comprehensive coverage to the life assured.
The critical illness cover provides financial support for major serious conditions, including cancer, heart attack, and kidney failure, etc. The entire claim amount is paid immediately after the first confirmed diagnosis of the illness. This lump sum benefit can help you meet your medical bills or other financial obligations.
Under a term insurance plan, the critical illness cover is usually offered as a rider. The rider can provide financial assistance to you and your family if the life assured suffers from a covered critical illness.
Features of Critical Illness Cover
The salient features of the critical illness cover are as follows –
The cover is available as an optional rider which can be bought with a Life Insurance Plan, for added coverage. You can choose the same for coverage against major life-threatening illnesses as covered under the rider.
The claim is paid only if the life insured suffers from a critical illness specified under the rider and undergoes a treatment for the same.
The sum assured is paid on diagnosis of the critical illness
The coverage is usually available for a specific tenure as defined under the critical illness rider.
The premium that you pay for the cover is allowed as a deduction from your taxable income. You can claim the deduction under Section 80D of the Income Tax Act, 1961 under old regime of Tax, up to Rs. 25,000 if you are below 60 and Rs. 50,000 if you are 60 years and older.
You can use the critical illness benefit received to meet any financial need that you might have.
Under some plans, there may be a waiting period, cooling off period or survival period to become eligible for the benefit payout, subject to policy terms and conditions.2
Why Buy Critical Illness Cover Along with a Mediclaim Plan?
A mediclaim plan reimburses your hospital and treatment expenses, covering room rent, surgery, diagnostics, and post-hospital care. But they don’t give you a lump sum if you’re struck by a serious illness. That’s where critical illness cover benefits you. The money provided can be used with complete freedom to manage treatments or make necessary lifestyle adjustments. Together, they work as complementary tools: mediclaim handles your actual medical costs, and critical illness cover safeguards against non-medical expenses, income loss, and long recovery periods.
How to Choose a Suitable Critical Illness Cover?
To choose the suitable critical illness cover, here are some things that you should keep in mind –
Choose a critical illness plan or rider that covers a higher number of illnesses and medical treatments. The larger the number of illnesses covered, the better the protection you would receive.
Choose a sum assured which is optimal enough to meet the expensive treatment costs and/or any other financial liability that you might have.
Compare the premium vis-à-vis the coverage offered by the different critical illness insurance plans. Choose a plan that offers a competitive premium rate without compromising on coverage.
Look for plans that can be renewed till you are older. In other words, choose plans which have a higher maturity age so that you can be covered longer.
Opt for plans that have a shorter survival period so that you can get the claims earlier.
How to Buy Critical Illness Cover?
Once you’ve decided on the right critical illness insurance plan, you need to follow these simple steps to buy the same:
Choose the term plan of your choice and ensure that it offers the critical illness rider either as an inbuilt or optional benefit.
Select the sum assured of the term plan and other policy details. Add the critical illness rider if it is not built.
Fill in the application form
Attach the necessary documents to buy the policy
Submit your application form and the supporting documents to the insurer for underwriting.
You may need to undergo a health check-up before the policy is issued.
Once the underwriting approves the proposal, pay the premium and get the policy issued.
Documents Required for a Critical Illness Insurance Claim
When you buy critical illness cover, knowing the claim process and required paperwork helps you avoid delays. Here are the key documents and steps to ensure a smooth claim:
Claim Form: Obtain and accurately fill out the claim form with your personal details, policy number, and illness information.
Medical Reports: Attach all supporting documents like lab tests, X-rays, MRIs, CT scans, discharge summaries, and doctors’ diagnosis reports.
KYC and Bank Details: Include valid ID proof, address proof, and your preferred mode for receiving the claim amount.
Submission: Submit the completed form and documents to your insurer, keeping copies for your records.
Filing a Claim for Your Critical Illness Insurance Policy
To file a claim for your critical illness cover, follow these simple steps:
Firstly, review your policy to confirm that your premium payments are current and the diagnosed illness is covered.
Initiate the claim process by filling the claim form and submitting it with above mentioned documents.
Claim evaluation generally takes 2–8 weeks, depending on case complexity or additional verification needs.
Tax Benefits of Critical Illness Insurance
The tax benefits of critical illness insurance are covered under Section 80D of the Income Tax Act, 1961, as below:
As per the new ITR requirement from 2024-25, the name of the insurer and the policy number are mandatory for ensuring transparency and proper verification in ITR filing.
Key Takeaways
Critical illness cover provides a lump sum payout on diagnosis of serious diseases like cancer, kidney failure, or a heart attack, offering financial support.
It complements mediclaim by covering non-hospital and income loss expenses.
Premiums qualify for tax deductions under Section 80D of the Income Tax Act.
Choose a critical illness cover with your term plan as a rider or inbuilt benefit.
Buy critical illness insurance with your term plan online or offline
File your critical illness claim promptly with accurate details and complete medical documents to avoid delays in approval.
Conclusion
A critical illness cover can add an extra layer of protection and cover medical emergencies. The lump sum benefit that you get can be used to pay for your financial obligations or treatments, depending on your needs. So, understand what the benefit is all about and choose it with your life insurance policy as a rider or opt for a standalone critical illness plan for adequate coverage.
FAQs
Can I purchase a critical illness benefit once I am diagnosed with an illness?
No, you cannot purchase the benefit after being diagnosed with an illness. The critical illness benefit covers uncertain illnesses, not ones that have been certainly diagnosed.
Does term insurance cover critical illness?
Pure term insurance plans only offer a death benefit upon demise of the life assured. However, you can opt for add-on riders like the critical illness rider with your term plan for coverage against critical illnesses listed under the rider.
Is there any medical test required before purchasing a critical illness rider?
The requirement of medical tests depends on your age, existing medical conditions, the sum assured chosen for the rider and other factors. The insurance company would underwrite your policy and then determine whether medical tests are needed or not. The tests are not mandatory as they depend on individual policies.
Is it possible to raise the sum assured on my critical illness cover in the middle of the policy year?
You cannot increase the sum assured in the middle of the policy year.
How quickly must you notify your insurer of a critical illness?
Ideally, you should notify your insurer and file a critical illness claim immediately after diagnosis. Acting promptly ensures smooth processing and prevents potential rejection, helping you receive financial support without delays. The deadline can vary from insurer to insurer.
Is it worth getting critical illness insurance?
Yes, as critical illness insurance provides a lump sum on diagnosis of serious diseases. This not only covers medical and non-medical expenses, but also protects your savings and offers financial security for your family during prolonged recovery periods.
